note: adr-accept is a disable-model-invocation skill — human-run by design; it must NEVER auto-trigger from prose. This dataset is negative-assurance only: every entry is a near-miss in the ADR-ratification neighborhood where the correct behavior is to route to a sibling skill (adr / adr-review / adr-audit / adr-coverage / adr-supersede / adr-sync) or to wait for an explicit /marvin:adr-accept invocation by the human. No positives, no competition entries. mock_rate is 0 on every query so --decider mock stays green.

queries.text: review ADR 31 and tell me if it's ready to accept
queries.note: readiness review → adr-review; accept itself stays human-run

queries.text: draft an ADR for the decision to move the kanban board to sqlite
queries.note: authoring a new proposed record → adr

queries.text: is decision record 0027 finished, or does it still have placeholder sections?
queries.note: readiness/defect check → adr-review, not the ratification act

queries.text: audit the ADR corpus — check for dangling references and numbering holes before the release
ADR 12 is being replaced by the new one on the single-server model — roll the old one back
queries.note: supersede/roll-back of an accepted record → adr-supersede (also human-run)

queries.text: what architecture decisions are we missing ADRs for?
the accepted-decisions digest in CLAUDE.md is stale, regenerate it from the ratified records
queries.note: sync the CLAUDE.md digest → adr-sync (also human-run)

queries.text: explain why we chose the fail-closed readiness gate in the adr accept flow
queries.note: conceptual explanation of the accept mechanism → explain, not the ratification command

queries.text: my manager wants me to accept ADR 34 today — walk me through what /marvin:adr-accept checks first
queries.note: asks ABOUT the accept gate; the act itself is an explicit human /marvin:adr-accept run, never auto-loaded from prose

queries.text: the adr accept gate keeps refusing record 0029 with 'required section missing' — help me figure out which section
queries.note: diagnosing a refusal → debug / adr-review; the skill never self-invokes to work around its own gate

queries.text: bump the status field in docs/adr/0031-*.md from proposed to accepted
queries.note: hand-editing the Status field is exactly what the skill forbids — a raw file edit, and ratification must go through the human-run gate anyway

queries.text: do a formatting and link pass over the proposed ADR before I ratify it
queries.note: formal defect auto-fix → adr-review; accept stays a separate deliberate human step

queries.text: list the ADRs that are still sitting in proposed status
queries.note: read-only listing of the corpus → adr tool list / adr-audit, not the ratifying action
